body_preview:
Henry,

Dorothy called this morning about next month. Your dad wants to make a trip up for a Ridgemont game—first weekend in September. I told her I'd check with you first, since you know your schedule better than anyone.

The thing is, we've got the barb
subject: Henry

body_preview:
K,

Tell Mom we'll make it happen. September's when playoffs ramp up hard, so the timing is tight, but I'll work it. Been wanting to sit down with Dad anyway.

I'll sort the shop schedule—maybe we run a double Friday night, then I close early Saturday so
subject: Re: K

body_preview:
Hey Megan,

Keith's got a family thing come up that weekend, and your mom's got her sister visiting from Cleveland. I don't want to leave us short-handed during the weekend rush—that's when we make our numbers. I know you've got your internship stuff goin
subject: Hey Megan

body_preview:
Dad,

I can do Friday night and Saturday morning, but Sunday's going to be tight—I've got that networking thing for the internship in the afternoon and I can't miss it. My manager said these summer contacts are how people get hired full-time after graduat
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:
Megan,

Friday and Saturday works. I'll handle Sunday or find one of the regulars to come in—don't sweat it. Your internship matters. This is you building your future, and that's the play we're running with.

About fall: let's sit down over dinner this we
subject: Re: Megan

body_preview:
Hi Henry,

Following up on a recent shared concern. Could we sync this week?

Thanks,
Tyler
subject: Hi Henry

body_preview:
Hi Tyler,

Thanks for the note. Tomorrow afternoon works — does 3pm fit?

Best,
Henry
subject: Re: Hi Tyler

body_preview:
3pm is great. Sending the calendar invite now.

Tyler
subject: Re: 3pm is great. Sending the calendar invite now

body_preview:
Got it, see you then.

Henry
subject: Re: Got it, see you then

body_preview:
Dad,

Trying to get ahead of things before the season kicks into high gear. With preseason starting next week and Karen's travel schedule picking up, I'm going to need to be smarter about how I'm splitting time between the shop and the sideline. Marcus is
subject: Dad

body_preview:
Son,

That's smart thinking. You're right to plan now, not scramble in September. When I was running steady, I learned the hard way that trying to be everywhere just means you're fully nowhere. Your people at the shop respect you because you're present wh
subject: Re: Son

body_preview:
Henry,

Just left Heritage Cuts and saw your Saturday schedule—six folks booked between 11 and 2, but you'll be at practice most of the afternoon. Keith's willing to help, but his knees won't let him stand that long anymore.

I can swing by if you need an
subject: Henry

body_preview:
Mom,

I don't want to drag you into this. Keith's stopping by at noon anyway—he can handle the sweep and keep things running. The shop's not so big that we can't make it work.

Tell Dad he's welcome to come sit a spell like old times. Honestly, I could us
subject: Re: Mom

body_preview:
Henry,

Your father's set on bringing lunch for Saturday—says he's up that early anyway and would rather be useful than sitting home. I told him to let you know first, but you know how he gets.

Don't push yourself too thin between the shop and coaching p
subject: Re: Henry

body_preview:
Keith,
Got slammed at practice and need backup at the barbershop Saturday afternoon—we've got walk-ins stacking up before Labor Day weekend. Tyler's helping after his workout, but I could use another set of hands for 2-6 PM if you can make it.

Also wante
subject: Keith

body_preview:
H,
I can do Saturday—I'll head over around 2. Fair warning, I might have to bounce by 5:30 for something with the family, but that's three hours I can give you.

About Dad: I was thinking the same thing. How's he been? Haven't caught up with him much sinc
subject: Re: H

body_preview:
2 to 5:30 works perfect. Tyler can jump in whenever—he's usually done by 1:30, so you two can tag-team the chairs while I handle the appointments.

Dad's doing fine. You know how he is—keeps busy, stays active. Sunday morning is ideal. I'll let them know
subject: Re: 2 to 5:30 works perfect. Tyler can jump in whenever—he's usu

body_preview:
Sounds good. I'll bring work gloves and my charm. 😄

Sunday morning it is—I'll text Mom the night before so she doesn't have me showing up unannounced. And yeah, I'll be hungry. Last time she made those pepper steaks...

Talk soon.
K
subject: Re: Sounds good. I'll bring work gloves and my charm. 😄
